_comment: HIGH-THROUGHPUT Arrow RecordBatch version of perftest_trade_etl. Targets ~10k msg/s by draining many Kafka trades per cycle into ONE columnar pyarrow.RecordBatch and running every stage as a single vectorized pass, with the immutable batch shared downstream by reference (zero per-hop copy). HETEROGENEOUS TRADES: individual Kafka trades may carry different attribute sets; only a few core attributes are always present. The ingest node (NormalizingArrowBatchingSubscriptionNode) normalizes every batch to a STABLE schema - typed core columns (trade_id/seq/symbol/side/quantity/price/currency) plus one JSON 'extras_json' column that losslessly carries every non-core attribute - so ragged input never drops attributes and never breaks the schema (unlike a raw from_pylist, which infers schema from the first row only and crashes on cross-row type conflicts). TOPOLOGY: linear, because RecordBatch fan-in is unsupported (a node may receive at most one batch edge); the original fan-out/fan-in (fees|risk|anomaly -> classify) is fused into a vectorized chain. Fan-OUT to the three sinks is fine. Sinks use ArrowFlatteningPublicationNode, which converts the batch back to one message per row so the external/broker contract is unchanged. Tuning for 10k/s: batch.max_size 5000 (~2 batches/s at 10k/s) and a deep subscriber queue; raise idle_poll a touch since latency is dominated by batch fill, not poll. Additive/opt-in: custom node + calculators are referenced by dotted path; the engine is untouched.
name: perftest_trade_etl_arrow
description: Kafka -> arrow normalize-ingest (stable schema + extras) -> FX -> notional -> fees -> risk -> classify -> anomaly -> summarize -> {file sink, kafka sink, kafka async sink}. Columnar/zero-copy high-throughput equivalent of perftest_trade_etl for heterogeneous trades.

config._idle_note: Deep queue so the batching ingest can drain large batches under load; latency at 10k/s is dominated by batch fill, not poll interval.

config._async_note: async_egress:true routes this publisher through the WAL-backed AsyncPublisher when egress.async.enabled=true, for async vs sync side-by-side benchmarking.
